rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF EUGENE J. MALINOWSKI
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside And Vacate Order Surcharging And Removing Administrator Pursuant To Ccp 663; And Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Granted. The portion of the April 12, 2019 Order imposing a surcharge is set aside and vacated. The issues of (a) whether to impose a surcharge and (b) the amount of any such surcharge are restored to calendar for hearing on July 30, 2019 at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204 of the San Francisco Superior Court. Any pleadings addressing these issues must be filed by July 23, 2019. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. Wednesday, June 12, 2019.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF EUGENE J. MALINOWSKI
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For New Trial Pursuant To Ccp 657; And Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: The motion for new trial is moot in light of the ruling granting the motion to vacate. Issues concerning imposition of a surcharge are restored to calendar for hearing on July 30, 2019 at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204 of the San Francisco Superior Court. Any party wishing to contest this tenatative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. Wednesday, June 12, 2019.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MAY FUNG MEI TAM TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Payment Of Cancellation Fee Compliance With Deposition Subpoenas For Sanctions And For Order Requiring Non Resident Petitioner To File And Undertaking To Secure Costs
rulings.Rulings: Granted in part and denied in part. The request for an undertaking is granted. Repondent has established a "reasonable possibility" of prevailing, and Petitioner resides outside California. Petitioner has not presented evidence justifying an exemption on the basis of financial hardship. Respondent has not shown a likelihood of recovering attorneys fees if he prevails. Petitioner must post an undertaking in the amount of $30,000, which the Court finds is a reasonable estimate of reasonably incurred costs on the basis of the parties' submissions. A status hearing is set for July 16, 2019 on the posting of the undertaking. The Court denies the request to order Petitioner to pay the translator's cancellation fee (without commenting on any right Respondent may have to recover the fee in a cost award or otherwise). The Court does not find that Petitioner's requested rescheduling was an abuse of the discovery process. The Court denies the request to order the third-party deponents to attend their now rescheduled depositions at which they have agreed to appear. The Court declines to strike the over-long opposition given the lack of prejudice but cautions Petitioner to comply with the Rules of Court on all future filings (including the requirement to provide courtesy copies to the Court of all filings). The Court declines to rule on the evidentiary objections because they go to matters not necessary to the Court's ruling on this motion. The Court denies the parties' respective requests for fees in light of their mixed results on this motion. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. Wednesday, June 12, 2019.
